paper_title: Analysis of Nanometer Spheres, Element Distribution, and Minerals in S. epidermidis Culture

methods.method_name: S. epidermidis Y2 Cell Preparation and Microscopy
methods.description: Procedure for preparing S. epidermidis Y2 bacterial cells for ultra-thin section analysis using various electron microscopy techniques (TEM, HRTEM, SAED, STEM) to study nanometer spheres and element distribution outside and inside the cells.

steps.description: Ten milliliters of S. epidermidis Y2 bacterial liquid was centrifuged at 3000 rpm for 5 min;
inputs: 10 mL S. epidermidis Y2 bacterial liquid

steps.description: the pellet was washed three times with phosphate buffer (Na 2 HPO 4 •12H 2 O 20.7472 g L −1 , NaH 2 PO 4 •2H 2 O 3.1167 g L −1 , pH 7.2) to remove the ingredients from the culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash bacterial pellet with phosphate buffer
inputs: Phosphate buffer (Na2HPO4•12H2O 20.7472 g L−1, NaH2PO4•2H2O 3.1167 g L−1, pH 7.2)

steps.description: The pellet was fixed by glutaraldehyde (2.5%, v/v) for a night,
then dehydrated gradually by acetone solution: 30% (15 min), −50% (15 min), −70% (15 min), −80% (15 min), −90% (15 min), −95% (15 min), −100% (15 min), −100% (15 min) and −100% (15 min).
steps.searchable_summary: Dehydrate fixed bacterial pellet using graded acetone series

steps.description: Finally, a 30% epoxy resin solution was added to embed the cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Embed dehydrated bacterial cells in epoxy resin

steps.description: The ultra-thin slices were analyzed with TEM (JEM-2100, JEOL, Tokyo, Japan) [60][61][62][63], HRTEM (H-7650, Hitachi, Tokyo, Japan) [64][65][66], SAED [58] and STEM (Tecnai G2 F20, FEI, Hillsboro, OR, USA) [67].
steps.searchable_summary: Perform electron microscopy analysis (TEM, HRTEM, SAED, STEM) on ultra-thin sections
inputs: Ultra-thin slices of embedded bacterial cells

methods.method_name: XRD Data Analysis of Precipitated Minerals
methods.description: Analysis of X-ray Diffraction (XRD) patterns obtained from minerals precipitated in bacterial culture media with different sources of magnesium (MgSO4 vs MgCl2) and varying Mg/Ca molar ratios, focusing on identifying mineral phases, comparing crystal cell density, and assessing crystallinity.

steps.description: Obtain XRD patterns from mineral samples precipitated in culture media with different magnesium sources and Mg/Ca ratios.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain XRD patterns from mineral samples
inputs: Mineral samples from culture media (control, experimental groups with MgSO4/MgCl2 at various Mg/Ca ratios)

steps.description: Identify mineral phases present in the XRD patterns by comparison with standard databases.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify mineral phases from XRD patterns

steps.description: Compare the identified mineral phases across different experimental conditions (e.g., MgSO4 vs MgCl2 media, different Mg/Ca ratios).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare mineral phases identified in XRD patterns across experimental conditions
inputs: Identified mineral phases for each sample/condition
outputs: Comparison of mineral phase presence/absence across conditions

steps.description: Analyze and compare crystal cell density based on features in the XRD patterns (e.g., peak positions).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze and compare crystal cell density from XRD data
outputs: Crystal cell density values for each sample/condition

steps.description: Analyze and compare crystallinity of minerals based on features in the XRD patterns (e.g., number and sharpness of peaks).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze and compare mineral crystallinity from XRD patterns

steps.description: Interpret the results to understand the effect of different magnesium sources on mineral phase, crystal cell density, and crystallinity.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret XRD analysis results to determine effect of magnesium source on mineral properties
outputs: Conclusions on the influence of MgSO4 vs MgCl2 on mineral formation and structure
